title: What I Eat in a Day (full breakdown)

chunks.title: Introduction to Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces the video as a follow-up to a previous one, where they plan to break down their daily animal-based diet using the Chronometer app. They will showcase two days of their eating habits and discuss nutrient details.
chunks.transcript: what's up you guys I did a video recently about what I eat in a day and I wanted to do a companion video to that video where I break down what I eat in a day on chronometer and show you all of the nutrients in my animal-based diet I have two days of my animal-based eating recorded here and I'm gonna go through all of that on chronometer and show you what I eat in a day on that app with all the nutrients the macros Etc at the new website at carnivoremd.com there's a calculator there that's free where you can put in your weight your activity level and it'll give you a suggestion of macros to create an animal-based Diet before I dive in I want to show you a couple of clips from my what I eat in the day video and then we'll go to

chunks.title: Detailed Breakdown of Meals
chunks.summary: The speaker describes their daily meals, emphasizing the importance of incorporating organ meats, fruits, and raw dairy. They mention specific meals including smoothies, ribeye steaks, and local organic fruits, while noting their preferred eating times.
chunks.transcript: chronometer I'm going to show you what I eat in a day raw organic honey drink a coconut and then for my first full meal of the day it starts with a smoothie then I have about a pound of grass-fed ground beef the sauce forcing of the meat is very important to me organs meat fruit honey raw dairy basically twice a day at both of my main meals a little bit of fruit is a snack today I'm having some local organic pineapple it's about 4 30. I like to finish eating by 5 PM grass fed ribeye steak you can make ground beef the majority of what you eat also whole package from heart and soil supplements with dinner that has desiccated testicle in it I'm gonna have some raw butter with my steak the rest of that organic pineapple from the local fruit guy it breaks down into around 200 grams of protein around 250 grams of carbohydrates and around 150 to 160 grams of fat so that's what I eat in a day I never get sick of this I look forward to it every day time for dinner

chunks.title: Nutrient Composition from Chronometer
chunks.summary: The speaker transitions to using Chronometer to analyze their nutrient intake over a specific day. They provide insights into the caloric intake and nutritional breakdown including protein, fat, and carbohydrate content, highlighting their high caloric consumption.
chunks.transcript: tonight we'll do it all again tomorrow all right two chronometer we go so you can see here this is a sample day that I put October the 10th 2022 and everything I ate in that day is here I ate a banana with some home milk a raw pineapple that was part of my kefir smoothie in the morning I have some salt that's low microplastic sea salt I eat honey in the morning before I Surf and also a little bit in the smoothie I have 80 20 ground beef I ate about 300 grams in the morning 300 grams at night I have some raw liver about an ounce a day I eat many tablespoons of raw butter I have beef heart raw or cooked I have some bone broth they call it boned broth I don't know if it's bone broth or boned broth I have some coconuts that I drink a papaya have some heart and soil supplements in the morning on that day I had some bone marrow and liver I think it's pretty cool that chronometer actually lets you put heart and soil in there I had some bone marrow and the only bone marrow on chronometer was Caribou so that's what I put there ground beef 300 more grams at dinner a raw egg yolk at some point during the day a plantain that I cooked with some more butter and some sea salt that's about my whole day of eating and you'll see that in the video on what I eat in a day chronometer also lets you put in a couple of hours of Surfing which I rated as hard now the first thing you'll notice here is that the amount of calories I have is enormous 3791 calories and I only burned uh 2535 calories that's I guess somehow chronometer is calculating my basal metabolic rate by these calculations I should be gaining weight every day to the tune of multiple pounds of fat per week which is not the

chunks.title: Vitamins and Minerals in the Diet
chunks.summary: The discussion turns to vitamins and minerals, with the speaker emphasizing the benefits of organ meats for nutrients like riboflavin and B12. They address inaccuracies in Chronometer's data regarding vitamin levels such as vitamin D and E.
chunks.transcript: case because I am five nine and a half I weigh 164 to 165 pounds every single day I weigh myself after I poop in the morning so I have a higher basal metabolism than chronometer thinks I do which is not surprising I'll talk about that a little bit in this video you can see total energy the protein breakdown here is 176 grams right about one gram of protein per pound of body weight which is what I recommend my carbs were 247 grams and my fat was 230 grams of fat there's no seed oils here because sea oils are [ __ ] you guys know it so let's look at the nutrients here you can see in terms of the vitamins at least the ones that chronometer shows I'm fine on thymine I have lots of riboflavin heart and liver are the best sources of riboflavin and so I want to get the organs for the riboflavin if you're not getting organs you're probably not getting enough riboflavin critical for methylation there's really good research showing that the MTHFR enzyme in people who have MTHFR polymorphisms works better if you get a little more riboflavin than the RDA I happen to have a 677c to T polymorphism in my own MTHFR and so I like to get a lot of riboflavin in my diet it it's saying niacin is red because it's saying I'm getting too much that's just because I'm eating meat but all these are green so I'm getting plenty of B6 I'm getting plenty of B5 pantothenic acid I'm getting a massive amount of B12 no vegans can say that getting lots of folate I'm getting tons of vitamin A from the liver I'm getting lots of vitamin C from the fruit that I eat and it says I'm not getting enough vitamin D well I don't want to get vitamin D for my Foods I get vitamin D from the Sun and it's telling me I'm not getting enough vitamin E which is false because when I've done my blood work my vitamin E levels are just fine I think that chronometer and the databases that are used for this are often

chunks.title: Omega Fatty Acids and Dietary Considerations
chunks.summary: The speaker explains the importance of maintaining low omega-6 fatty acid intake and discusses the sources and effects of these fats. They criticize seed oils and their potential negative impact on metabolic health.

chunks.title: Comparison of Daily Caloric Intake
chunks.summary: The speaker compares two days of eating, finding consistent patterns in caloric intake and confirming that they do not gain weight despite a caloric surplus. They highlight the role of metabolic health and lifestyle in their stable weight.
chunks.transcript: their job so this is not a good thing yet another reason to avoid seed oils in your diet so that is a quick breakdown for you guys of what I eat in a day looking at chronometer looking at the nutrients in my diet which are all adequate on this diet there's none of the nutrients that are missing many of the nutrients in there are actually left out there's no calculation on chronometer for anserine taurine carnitine creatine there's nothing in there for biotin which I'm going to get from liver there's nothing in there for things like coenzyme Q10 which is rich in muscle meat and heart so chronometer is a pretty good place to start but I wanted to show you how my animal-based diet number one breaks down in terms of macros again you can go to carnivoremd.com to calculate what macros might be good for you on an animal-based diet based on your goals your weight how active you are and you can see that I have a caloric Surplus every day according to what most people might think my metabolism would be but there are multiple factors about the way that I'm living that I believe allow me to defy the norm whether that's an avoidance of seed oils and a thyroidal hormone axis that keeps the basal metabolism higher than a quote-unquote normal not so normal person who is slightly metabolically unhealthy or it's the fact that I get plenty of vitamin D or it's just the fact that when you eat certain foods like this your metabolism works well I'll just share one study in this short video what we know very clearly is that Ultra processed diets obviously I'm eating an unprocessed diet cause excess calorie intake and weight gain this is a inpatient randomized controlled trial of AD libetum food intake from Kevin Hall's group it's such an interesting trial because they had 20 weight stable adults they put one half of the group for two weeks on an ultra processed diet and one half of the group on an unprocessed diet as they see here meals were designed to be matched for presented calories energy density macronutrients sugar and sodium and fiber and what happened well the people who had the ultra processed foods just ate more because they were consistently more hungry they kept looking for more food you can see here in this basic graph that the body weight of the ultra processed group went up about one kilogram in two weeks so about two pounds and the unprocessed group just lost weight easily so there's a lot to be said about this the ultra process group consistently ate more calories throughout the two weeks and the unprocessed group ate less calories as you saw from micronometer I'm eating quite a bit of calories more than either of the groups in this trial and yet my weight is stable and I remain very lean

chunks.title: Conclusion on Animal-Based Nutrition
chunks.summary: The speaker concludes by summarizing the benefits of an animal-based diet, asserting that it provides all necessary nutrients while debunking myths around calorie intake and weight management. They encourage viewers to embrace unprocessed foods.
chunks.transcript: so the takeaway from this is number one an animal-based diet has all the nutrients you need to thrive number two the macros I presented here are how I do it number three I believe that if you eat unprocessed foods and real food your metabolism will work well and you will lose weight effortlessly all right guys
